stimulus: In evaluating the compositions of Brazilian musician Hermeto Pascoal, ______blank have overlooked his equally profound engagement with Indigenous Amazonian musical traditions such as Kuarup chants.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

answerOptions.content: Pascoal's use of jazz harmonic vocabulary has been the focus of many scholars, who
there are many scholars who have focused on Pascoal's use of jazz harmonic vocabulary, but they
the focus of many scholars has been on Pascoal's use of jazz harmonic vocabulary; they
many scholars have focused on Pascoal's use of jazz harmonic vocabulary but

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The convention being tested is subject-modifier placement. This choice makes the noun phrase "many scholars" the subject of the sentence and places it immediately after the modifying phrase "In evaluating…Pascoal." In doing so, this choice clearly establishes that it is the scholars—and not another noun in the sentence—who evaluate Pascoal's compositions.

Choice A is incorrect because it results in a dangling modifier. The placement of the noun phrase "Pascoal's…vocabulary" immediately after the modifying phrase illogically suggests that his use of jazz harmonic vocabulary is what evaluates Pascoal's compositions. Choice B is incorrect because it results in a dangling modifier. The placement of the function word "there" immediately after the modifying phrase illogically suggests that "there" is what evaluates Pascoal's compositions. Choice C is incorrect because it results in a dangling modifier. The placement of the noun phrase "the focus…scholars" immediately after the modifying phrase illogically suggests that the scholars' focus is what evaluates Pascoal's compositions.
